originalAlarm.description: Value stored to '__buf_978' is never read

sanitizedDescription: Value stored to 'buf' is never read
model:
(define-fun CONFIG_FEATURE_CLEAN_UP () Bool
  false)
(define-fun _STDLIB_H () Bool
  false)
(define-fun __malloc_and_calloc_defined () Bool
  false)
reasoning: When CONFIG_FEATURE_CLEAN_UP=F, buf is assigned at L26 from malloc(bufsize) but the cleanup block (L29-33) is absent, so buf is never read or freed — genuine dead store and memory leak. The VBDB // ERROR at L31 marks a different bug (freeing potentially uninitialized buf when CLEAN_UP=T). New bug. PC = !CLEAN_UP AND !_STDLIB_H AND !__malloc_and_calloc_defined: the restrictive library macros (!_STDLIB_H, !__malloc_and_calloc_defined) over-constrain the PC — the bug fires in all !CLEAN_UP configs, not just when stdlib.h internals are in a non-standard state.
